commit.message:
fix: unify shell.showItemInFolder path handling for VM and host paths

Refactored to handle both /sessions/... VM paths and direct SESSIONS_BASE/...
host paths with the same validation logic:

- Extract session root from either path format
- Validate session name (reject ., .., /)
- Check containment lexically before canonicalizing
- Walk up to nearest ancestor within session bounds on missing paths

commit.message:
fix: record all mount failure paths, handle rwd mode

The required-mount failure check was missing several early-exit paths:

1. Mode check expanded: 'rw' -> includes('w') to handle 'rwd' mode
   (.claude commonly arrives with mode: 'rwd')

2. Directory creation failure: now records in failedMounts before continue

3. Missing host path (read-only): now records failure instead of silent skip

4. Stale directory at mount point: now records failure instead of silent skip
   (can't create symlink over existing directory)

All failure paths now push to failedMounts, so required mount failures
(.claude) properly trigger session setup failure.

commit.message:
fix: fail fast on required mount failures, preserve signal exits, surface watch errors

1. createMountSymlinks: Track failed mounts, return false if required mounts
   (.claude) fail. Non-required failures logged as warnings.

2. Process exit: Preserve null exit code for signaled exits instead of
   coercing to 0. Caller can distinguish clean exit (code=0) from signal
   termination (code=null, signal=SIGTERM).

3. files.watch: Throw on error instead of silently returning dummy watcher.
   Caller can now catch and handle watch setup failures.

commit.message:
fix: restore IPC object contract, limit non-session fallback to parent only

IPC contract fix:
- getSyntheticIPCResponse and ClaudeVM handler return { running, exitCode }
- App expects e?.running, bare boolean made all processes look stopped
- swiftStub synthetic methods still return boolean (direct stub API)

Non-session fallback fix:
- openFile/revealFile for host paths now only fall back to immediate parent
- If parent doesn't exist, return false instead of walking to / or /home
- Prevents /definitely/missing/path from degrading to /

commit.message:
fix: return existing ancestor directly in shell.showItemInFolder fallback

The fallback branch was doing:
  resolvedPath = path.join(fs.realpathSync(current), path.basename(resolvedPath))

This appends the original filename to the ancestor, creating a non-existent
path. For /sessions/s1/mnt/foo/bar/baz.txt where only .../foo exists, it
returned .../foo/baz.txt (doesn't exist) instead of .../foo.

Now returns the existing ancestor directory directly so the file manager
opens a valid location.

Known limitation: stdin translation can still rewrite prose containing
literal "/sessions/..." quotes. Distinguishing path fields from prose
requires JSON schema awareness, which was the original structured rewriting
approach that caused ordering issues. Accepting this edge case for now.

commit.message:
fix: handle mixed payloads and reject . in session names

Two fixes from review:

1. stdin: Remove early SESSIONS_BASE bailout that skipped all translation
   when payload contained any translated path. Mixed payloads like
   {"cwd":"<host>","path":"/sessions/..."} now correctly translate the
   VM path while leaving the host path untouched. The regex patterns
   specifically match "/sessions/ which won't match SESSIONS_BASE paths.

2. shell.showItemInFolder: Reject '.' as session name in addition to '..'.
   /sessions/./other-session/file.txt would resolve sessionRoot to the
   entire sessions tree, defeating per-session boundary enforcement.

commit.message:
fix: tighten stdin translation and add post-realpath boundary check

Two security fixes from code review:

1. writeToProcess stdin translation was overbroad - blindly replacing
   /sessions/ anywhere would corrupt prose and double-translate paths.
   Now only translates JSON string values (matches "/sessions/ and
   "\/sessions\/ patterns) and skips if SESSIONS_BASE already present.

2. shell.showItemInFolder only checked session root boundary in the
   fallback branch. Paths like /sessions/<session>/../../etc/passwd
   would normalize outside the session tree via realpathSync before
   the guard ever ran. Now validates:
   - Session name format before path.join (no .. traversal)
   - Post-realpathSync result is still under session root

commit.message:
fix: restore minimal stdin path translation, bound shell.showItemInFolder fallback

Verification found the raw stdin passthrough revert breaks real /sessions/...
paths sent over stdin. The /sessions symlink points to a different tree than
LocalAgentModeSessions/sessions, so passthrough hands the CLI broken paths.

Restore minimal path translation in writeToProcess() using simple string
replacement. This avoids the full JSON parsing/rewriting that may have caused
ordering issues while still translating /sessions/... to SESSIONS_BASE.

Also bound the shell.showItemInFolder ancestor walk to the session root so it
can no longer escape to unrelated directories like /home/zack.

commit.message:
feat: desktop helper fallbacks, uploads preservation, ComputerUseTcc stubs

Desktop helpers (openFile, revealFile):
- Resolve symlinks via canonicalizeResolvableHostPath before opening
- Fall back to nearest existing ancestor directory when target is missing
- revealFile distinguishes files (nautilus --select) from directories
  (xdg-open) for correct file manager behavior

Uploads mount symlink creation:
- Preserve stale uploads directory contents before replacing with a
  symlink by moving entries to the host uploads path
- Handle cross-filesystem moves (EXDEV) via copyFileSync+unlinkSync
  fallback, with per-entry error isolation

ComputerUseTcc IPC handlers:
- Return stable denied state for macOS TCC permissions gate on Linux
  (accessibility, screenCapture) to prevent missing-handler errors on
  newer asar versions
- Register via both ipcMain.handle intercept and _invokeHandlers
  fallback for full coverage

Other:
- Implement get_app_info_for_file via xdg-mime query in native stub
- Add vm.isProcessRunning delegate and class method
- Improve envVar translation error message formatting

commit.message:
fix: replace blind writeToProcess regex with structured JSON rewriting

The old writeToProcess() blindly replaced all /sessions/ occurrences
in stdin data with SESSIONS_BASE, mangling user prose that happened to
contain that literal text and producing session-alias paths instead of
canonical host paths.

New approach parses newline-delimited JSON lines and rewrites VM paths
only in known path-bearing keys (cwd, path, filePath, args, etc.).
Non-JSON lines fall back to the regex as a safety net. Adds trace
logging when paths are actually rewritten for debuggability.

commit.message:
fix: harden path helpers per review findings

Address six issues from code review:

1. canonicalizeHostPath now throws on raw /sessions/ VM paths,
   enforcing the documented invariant that callers must translate first
2. CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR translation failure hard-fails the spawn instead of
   silently passing the untranslated VM path (original transcript-loss
   bug class)
3. spawn() destructures stdio from options and reorders the spread so
   cwd/env/stdio are always authoritative ({...safeOptions, cwd, env,
   stdio}) — prevents Object.assign bypass
4. files.list returns paths using the caller's original prefix instead
   of the canonicalized path, preventing UI prefix-match breakage
5. All silent catch blocks now trace warnings for debuggability
6. spawnSync logs when options.env is silently dropped

Adds canonicalizePathForHostAccess() to route VM paths through strict
validation and host paths through canonicalizeHostPath, used by files.*
and spawn cwd resolution. Relaxes mount creation to soft-skip (with
warning) when additionalMounts is present but no session VM path is
found, avoiding hard failures on non-session spawns.

commit.message:
fix: canonicalize session mount alias paths to real host paths

The Swift stub translates VM-internal /sessions/... paths to
SESSIONS_BASE/... but never resolves through the mount symlinks
(e.g. .../mnt/zack -> /home/zack) back to canonical host paths.
This causes the CLI to run with a CWD like .../LocalAgentModeSessions/
sessions/<name>/mnt/zack, so the model sees and emits those long
alias paths instead of /home/zack/...

Add two helpers with strict separation of concerns:
- translateVmPathStrict(): validates and translates /sessions/...
  paths to SESSIONS_BASE, preserving all existing traversal rejection
- canonicalizeHostPath(): resolves symlinks via realpathSync with
  ancestor-walk fallback for nonexistent write targets

Surfaces patched:

1. spawn() options merge bypass (security): Object.assign({ cwd, env },
   options) allowed options.cwd/options.env to overwrite sanitized
   values. Destructure and discard cwd/env from raw options so
   filterEnv() output and canonicalized cwd cannot be bypassed.

2. vm.spawn() args (line 978): canonicalize translated --add-dir and
   other /sessions/ args so the CLI receives real host paths.

3. vm.spawn() sharedCwdPath (line 1009): canonicalize so the CLI CWD
   is /home/zack rather than .../mnt/zack.

4. spawn() final cwd (line 1296): canonicalize the fallback chain
   (sharedCwdPath || options.cwd || process.cwd()) to catch all
   branches.

5. vm.readFile() / vm.writeFile() / vm.mountPath(): replace inline
   validation blocks with translateVmPathStrict + canonicalizeHostPath
   two-step. Same security checks, now with symlink resolution.

6. desktop.openFile / revealFile / previewFile: replace raw
   SESSIONS_BASE join with two-step pattern. Non-VM paths also
   canonicalized so UI file actions resolve mount aliases.

7. this.files.* (read, write, exists, stat, list, watch): canonicalize
   incoming paths. files.list() returns canonical paths in its path
   field instead of propagating alias paths.

8. spawnSync(): same options-merge fix as spawn() for parity — strip
   cwd/env from raw options, canonicalize cwd.

9. claude-native revealInFinder: add inline realpathSync fallback
   (separate module, no shared helper).

Not touched (by design):
- CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR env var translation: intentionally resolves through
  .claude mount symlink to asar session storage dir. Canonicalizing
  would risk breaking transcript persistence.
- sdk_bridge.js: dead spawn path, not currently invoked.
- frame-fix-wrapper.js FileSystem overrides: already use realpathSync.
- writeToProcess(): deferred to follow-up (added TODO comment). The
  blind /sessions/ regex replacement is fragile but the primary leak
  is fixed at spawn args/cwd.

commit.message:
fix: graceful shutdown on Linux window managers

The asar's close/quit handlers check isMac() and swallow close events
when process.platform is "darwin" (which we spoof). This left the
Electron process running after WM close, with stale SingletonLock
files blocking relaunch.

Add shutdown handling to frame-fix-wrapper.js:
- browser-window-created hook patches windows with a close listener
  that schedules app.exit(0) via setImmediate
- window-all-closed handler as safety net
- SIGTERM/SIGHUP/SIGINT signal handlers for kill/systemd/terminal
- Uses app.exit() (uncancellable) instead of app.quit()

Fixes silent relaunch failure on Hyprland, GNOME, KDE, Sway, i3.

subclass swap and Object.defineProperty attempt never took effect.

is the working path and is kept.

Also drops the orphaned OriginalBrowserWindow variable left over from
the previous removal.

commit.message:
refactor: track ipc-handler-setup.js at repo root

The IPC handler setup file was previously only in linux-app-extracted/
(gitignored build dir), which meant fixes were lost on fresh extracts.

Changes:
- Copy linux-app-extracted/ipc-handler-setup.js to repo root as tracked source
- Update test-launch.sh to copy ipc-handler-setup.js before packing (mirrors Swift stub pattern)
- Add ipc-handler-setup.js to asar repack trigger condition
- Remove ipc-handler-setup.js from .gitignore

This follows the established pattern:
  tracked source (root) → copied to linux-app-extracted/ → packed into asar

Fixes the issue where install.sh's DMG extraction would overwrite custom
IPC handlers with vanilla code, breaking dynamic UUID discovery and custom
plugin support.

commit.message:
fix: skip asar mounts in Cowork sessions

On macOS, app.getAppPath() returns a directory inside the .app bundle.
On Linux, it returns the packed app.asar file. The app passes this as
an additionalMount and as --add-dir to Claude Code, which fails with
"app.asar is not a directory".

Skip .asar mounts in createMountSymlinks and filter out --add-dir
args pointing to .asar files in spawn().

commit.message:
fix: dynamic eipc UUID discovery and i18n path fix

IPC handlers were registered against hardcoded UUIDs that change
with each asar build. Replace with auto-detection by intercepting
ipcMain.handle() to discover UUIDs as the asar registers its own
handlers, then back-fill our handlers for each new UUID seen.

Add CustomPlugins_$_listMarketplaces stub to suppress
"Unsupported platform: linux-x64" errors at startup.

Fix i18n JSON path in install.sh: app expects JSON files at
resources/i18n/*.json, not resources/*.json.

commit.message:
Merge pull request #26 from johnzfitch/fix/cowork-file-access

## Summary

  Security fix for Cowork file preview + critical fixes for system Electron
  compatibility and Wayland support.

  ### Changes

  1. **FileSystem readLocalFile IPC handler** (original scope):
     - Restricts reads to session directories only
     - Enforces 10MB size cap
     - Validates paths to prevent traversal attacks
     - Enables Cowork file preview in sidebar

  2. **7z exit code tolerance** (closes #25):
     - Tolerates exit code 1 during DMG extraction
     - /Applications symlink warnings are non-fatal
     - Only fails on exit codes 2+

  3. **Resources copy from DMG** (incorporates #27):
     - Copies Contents/Resources/* to linux-app-extracted/resources/
     - Prevents i18n crash: "ENOENT: resources/i18n/en-US.json"
     - Critical for system Electron (non-AppImage)

  4. **Wayland global shortcuts** (addresses #28):
     - Adds --enable-features=GlobalShortcutsPortal
     - Works on KDE Plasma and Hyprland
     - GNOME limitation documented in README

  ### Testing

  - ✅ File preview working in Cowork sidebar
  - ✅ Path traversal blocked (security)
  - ✅ DMG extraction succeeds despite symlink warnings
  - ✅ System Electron no longer crashes on startup
  - ✅ GlobalShortcutsPortal enabled on Wayland

  ### Closes
  - #25
  - Incorporates #27 (will close separately)

  ### Addresses
  - #28 (flag added; GNOME portal limitation documented)

commit.message:
fix: 7z exit code, resources copy, Wayland portal

Tolerate 7z exit code 1 during DMG extraction. The /Applications
symlink in macOS DMG files triggers "Dangerous link path" warnings
which produce exit code 1, but extraction still succeeds. Only
fail on exit codes 2+.

Copy Contents/Resources/* from DMG to linux-app-extracted/resources/
to prevent i18n crash on system Electron. The app reads i18n files
and tray icons from paths relative to the asar. These assets live
alongside app.asar in the DMG, not inside it. Without this copy,
claude-desktop crashes immediately with "ENOENT: resources/i18n/
en-US.json not found" when using system electron.

Add --enable-features=GlobalShortcutsPortal to both test-launch.sh
and PKGBUILD launcher for Wayland global shortcut support via
xdg-desktop-portal. Works on KDE/Hyprland; GNOME limitation
documented in README troubleshooting.

Closes #25
Incorporates #27
Addresses #28

commit.message:
security: restrict readLocalFile to session dirs, cap size

The IPC handler accepted arbitrary file paths and would read any file
on the host via symlink resolution. Adds path validation against the
four known session base directories (matching the isPathSafe pattern
used in the Swift stub). Also caps preview reads at 10 MB, fixes
binary detection to use NUL-byte sampling instead of a size heuristic,
and handles extensionless text files (Dockerfile, Makefile, etc.) by
basename lookup.

commit.message:
fix: launcher path bug, version-resilient cowork patch, download timeout

test-launch.sh used an absolute ASAR_FILE path that got doubled when
prepended with "./" — electron couldn't find the asar. Switch to
relative path since we already cd to SCRIPT_DIR.

Rewrite enable-cowork.py to auto-detect the platform-gate function
name (xPt in v1.1.3963, wj in older builds) via known patterns +
regex fallback with brace-counting, instead of matching a single
hardcoded string.

Add 5-minute timeout to the download size-stabilization loop in
install.sh to prevent infinite hang on stuck downloads.

Update README to remove hardcoded wj() references.

commit.message:
docs(oauth): correct compliance doc inaccuracies and address Feb 2026 policy

Three issues fixed:

1. Inaccurate "never forward OAuth tokens" claim: OAUTH-COMPLIANCE.md
   previously stated "Forward OAuth tokens to subprocesses: Never" and
   "No path exists for OAuth tokens to flow from the Electron process to
   Claude Code through our code." Both are false. CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN
   is explicitly exempted via CREDENTIAL_EXEMPT_KEYS and IS forwarded to
   the Claude Code CLI subprocess — as required for CLI authentication.

2. Summary table updated to accurately reflect that CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN
   is intentionally passed (Claude Desktop → Claude Code CLI, Anthropic →
   Anthropic), while all other credential-pattern env vars are blocked.

3. New section added addressing Anthropic's February 19, 2026 policy update
   that restricts OAuth token use to Claude Code and Claude.ai. Documents
   why this project's token passthrough (between two unmodified Anthropic
   apps) is distinct from the third-party tool usage the policy targets,
   along with a caveat that this is outside officially supported configs.

commit.message:
fix(install): redirect extract_app log output to stderr

extract_app() returns its result via stdout (echo), but log_info and
log_success also write to stdout. When called via $(), all stdout is
captured into the variable, corrupting the path with ANSI log lines.

Redirect log calls to stderr so the captured variable contains only
the Claude.app path. This was the root cause of "app.asar not found".

commit.message:
fix(install): browser-first DMG download — open claude.ai/download, watch XDG dir

Stop trying to curl the DMG directly. The download page is Cloudflare-
protected and any hardcoded versioned URL goes stale on each release.

Instead: open claude.ai/download in the user's browser via xdg-open,
then watch their XDG download directory (xdg-user-dir DOWNLOAD) for a
new Claude*.dmg. The installer resumes automatically once the file lands.

CLAUDE_DMG env var and positional arg still bypass all of this for
users who already have the file.

commit.message:
fix(oauth): narrow BLOCKED_ENV_KEY_PATTERN, add AuthRequest URL validation

Three review-driven fixes:

1. Narrow BLOCKED_ENV_KEY_PATTERN from generic terms (token, secret,
   credential) to OAuth-specific patterns (oauth_token, bearer_token,
   session_cookie). The broad pattern silently broke Bedrock/Vertex
   setups where AWS_SESSION_TOKEN / AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY are needed.
   CREDENTIAL_EXEMPT_KEYS for CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN is retained.

2. Add URL origin validation to AuthRequest.start() in claude-native
   stub before calling xdg-open. Matches the same allowlist used in
   Auth_$_doAuthInBrowser (claude.ai, auth/accounts/console.anthropic.com).

3. Document that filterEnvForSubprocess extra param is internal-only
   (never renderer/user-supplied), clarifying why it bypasses allowlist.

.gitignore: add entries for internal dev docs and local Claude settings.

security(oauth): harden env and token handling

Incorporate PR #10 security hardening into cowork-restored,
resolving the critical conflict where the PR's BLOCKED_ENV_KEY_PATTERN
would have silently re-introduced a 401 by blocking CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN.

Changes:
- Remove ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN from ENV_ALLOWLIST (was never needed)
- Add BLOCKED_ENV_KEY_PATTERN regex to gate credential-like additionalEnv keys
- Add CREDENTIAL_EXEMPT_KEYS set to explicitly pass CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN
  through filterEnv despite the regex (the legitimate CLI auth mechanism)
- Update addApprovedOauthToken to _token with full OAuth compliance JSDoc
- Add SDK_ENV_ALLOWLIST + filterEnvForSubprocess() in sdk_bridge.js
- Add ALLOWED_AUTH_ORIGINS + URL origin validation for doAuthInBrowser
- Add AuthRequest compliance documentation in claude-native stub
- Add OAUTH-COMPLIANCE.md full audit document (from PR #10)

releases.name: v3.0.6 — Cowork Stability & Mount Handling
releases.body:
### Fixes

- **Message ordering restored**: Fixed stdin path translation and session path handling to ensure cowork messages arrive in correct order
- **Shell reveal bounded**: `showItemInFolder` fallback now properly bounded to parent directory, returns existing ancestors directly for deleted paths
- **Mixed payload handling**: Properly handles mixed session/non-session payloads and rejects invalid session names containing `.`
- **Mount failure surfacing**: Fail fast on required mount failures, preserve signal exits, and surface watch errors to the user
- **Upload symlink tracking**: Record all mount failure paths including uploads symlink failures in `failedMounts`
- **Unified path handling**: `showItemInFolder` now works correctly for both VM-style `/sessions/` paths and direct host paths

### Changed

- Removed dead `findNearestExistingAncestor` helpers
- Tightened stdin translation with post-realpath boundary checks
- IPC object contract restored with proper type handling

### Commits

12 commits addressing path translation, mount handling, and IPC stability.

releases.name: v3.0.5 — Path Canonicalization & Desktop Helpers
releases.body:
# Release Notes — v3.0.5

## Canonicalize session mount alias paths to real host paths

### Problem

The Swift stub translates VM-internal `/sessions/...` paths to `SESSIONS_BASE/...` host paths, but never resolves the mount symlinks (e.g. `.../mnt/zack -> /home/zack`) back to canonical paths. The CLI and model see long alias paths like `~/.../LocalAgentModeSessions/sessions/<name>/mnt/zack/dev/project` instead of the real `/home/zack/dev/project`.

This caused:
- The model emitting unwieldy session-alias paths in tool calls and output
- File manager actions (Open File, Reveal in Finder) targeting the alias tree instead of the real location
- `files.*` API methods returning alias paths that could confuse path-prefix matching in the UI

### Security fix

`Object.assign({ cwd, env }, options)` in both `spawn()` and `spawnSync()` allowed raw `options.cwd` / `options.env` to silently overwrite the sanitized values produced by `filterEnv()` and path canonicalization. The options spread is now destructured so `cwd`, `env`, and `stdio` are always authoritative.

### Surfaces patched

| # | Surface | Risk |
|---|---------|------|
| 1 | `spawn()` options merge bypass | Security |
| 2 | `vm.spawn()` args | High |
| 3 | `vm.spawn()` sharedCwdPath | High |
| 4 | `spawn()` final cwd | High |
| 5 | `vm.readFile` / `writeFile` / `mountPath` | Medium |
| 6 | `desktop.open` / `reveal` / `preview` | Medium |
| 7 | `this.files.*` (6 methods) | Medium |
| 8 | `spawnSync()` options merge | Low |
| 9 | `claude-native revealInFinder` | Low |

### New helpers

- **`translateVmPathStrict()`** — validates and translates `/sessions/...` VM paths to `SESSIONS_BASE`, preserving all traversal rejection. Throws on invalid paths.
- **`canonicalizeHostPath()`** — resolves symlinks via `realpathSync` with ancestor-walk fallback for nonexistent write targets. Operates only on already-translated host paths.
- **`canonicalizePathForHostAccess()`** — routes VM paths through strict validation and host paths through canonicalization, used by `files.*` and spawn CWD resolution.

### Not touched (by design)

- **`CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` env translation** — intentionally resolves through `.claude` mount symlink to asar session storage. Canonicalizing risks breaking transcript persistence.
- **`sdk_bridge.js`** — dead spawn path, not invoked.
- **`frame-fix-wrapper.js`** — already uses `realpathSync()` everywhere.

---

## Structured writeToProcess JSON rewriting

The old `writeToProcess()` blindly replaced all `/sessions/` occurrences in stdin data with `SESSIONS_BASE`, mangling user prose that happened to contain that literal text and producing session-alias paths instead of canonical host paths.

New approach parses newline-delimited JSON lines and rewrites VM paths only in known path-bearing keys (`cwd`, `path`, `filePath`, `args`, etc.). Non-JSON lines fall back to the regex as a safety net. Adds trace logging when paths are actually rewritten.

---

## Path helper hardening

Addresses six issues from code review:

1. `canonicalizeHostPath` throws on raw `/sessions/` VM paths, enforcing the documented invariant that callers must translate first
2. `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` translation failure hard-fails the spawn instead of silently passing the untranslated VM path (the original transcript-loss bug class)
3. `spawn()` reorders the options spread so `cwd`/`env`/`stdio` are always authoritative
4. `files.list` returns paths using the caller's original prefix instead of the canonicalized path, preventing UI prefix-match breakage
5. All silent `catch` blocks now trace warnings for debuggability
6. `spawnSync` logs when `options.env` is silently dropped

---

## Desktop helper fallbacks & uploads preservation

<details>
<summary>Desktop helpers (openFile, revealFile)</summary>

- Resolve symlinks via `canonicalizeResolvableHostPath` before opening
- Fall back to nearest existing ancestor directory when target is missing
- `revealFile` distinguishes files (`nautilus --select`) from directories (`xdg-open`) for correct file manager behavior
</details>

<details>
<summary>Uploads mount symlink creation</summary>

- Preserve stale uploads directory contents before replacing with a symlink by moving entries to the host uploads path
- Handle cross-filesystem moves (`EXDEV`) via `cpSync` + `rmSync` fallback, with per-entry error isolation
- Handle directory entries in the EXDEV fallback (previously `copyFileSync` threw `EISDIR`)
</details>

<details>
<summary>ComputerUseTcc IPC handlers</summary>

- Return stable denied state for macOS TCC permissions gate on Linux (accessibility, screenCapture) to prevent missing-handler errors on newer asar versions
- Register via both `ipcMain.handle` intercept and `_invokeHandlers` fallback for full coverage
</details>

<details>
<summary>Other</summary>

- Implement `get_app_info_for_file` via `xdg-mime` query in native stub
- Add `vm.isProcessRunning` delegate and class method
- Improve envVar translation error message formatting
</details>

releases.name: v3.0.4 — Graceful shutdown on Linux window managers
releases.body:
# Release Notes — v3.0.4

## Graceful shutdown on Linux window managers

### Problem

Closing Claude Desktop via a window manager (Hyprland `killactive`, GNOME/KDE
close button, i3 `kill`, Sway, etc.) left the Electron process running in the
background. On the next launch, stale `SingletonLock` files blocked startup —
the app appeared to do nothing.

This affected all Linux desktop environments. The detached launcher (`nohup`)
introduced in v3.0.2 made it worse: without a terminal session to cushion
recovery from stale locks, relaunch silently failed.

### Root cause

We spoof `process.platform` as `"darwin"` so the asar's macOS-specific code
paths work. But this also activates macOS dock behavior:

1. The asar's BrowserWindow `close` handler checks `isMac()` and swallows the
   close event (hide-to-dock instead of quit)
2. The asar's `window-all-closed` handler checks `process.platform === "darwin"`
   and skips `app.quit()` (macOS convention: apps stay running after last window)
3. The asar's `before-quit` handler can cancel `app.quit()` calls

Result: the window disappears from the compositor but the process stays alive,
holding the singleton lock. Next launch sees the lock, can't connect to the
dead window's socket, and fails silently.

### Fix

Added graceful shutdown handling to `frame-fix-wrapper.js`:

- **`browser-window-created` hook**: patches every BrowserWindow with a `close`
  listener that schedules `app.exit(0)` via `setImmediate` (deferred so the
  asar's close handlers finish without "Object has been destroyed" errors)
- **`window-all-closed` handler**: registered before the asar's handler as a
  safety net — calls `app.exit(0)` if somehow triggered
- **Signal handlers**: `SIGTERM`, `SIGHUP`, `SIGINT` all call `app.exit(0)` so
  `kill`, `systemctl stop`, and terminal close all work
- Uses `app.exit()` instead of `app.quit()` because the asar's `before-quit`
  handler cancels quit attempts

### Testing

Verified on Hyprland (Wayland) with both `killactive` and `SIGTERM`:
- Window close: process exits, singleton files cleaned up, immediate relaunch works
- `kill <pid>`: clean exit
- `claude-desktop` from any directory: launches correctly

releases.name: v3.0.2 — Distro Hardening & Preflight Diagnostics
releases.body:
# v3.0.2 — Distro Hardening & Preflight Diagnostics **2026-02-27**
--- > [!IMPORTANT] > This release fixes a **breaking change** in Claude Desktop’s latest build: the asar now sends bare `claude` as the spawn command instead of `/usr/local/bin/claude`. Without this update, Cowork sessions fail to start. --- ## What Changed
Area Change Why
Spawn command Accept bare claude and vetted absolute paths, not just /usr/local/bin/claude Claude Desktop changed its spawn call; old stub rejected it as “unexpected command”
Password store Detect SecretService at runtime; fall back to --password-store=basic Hard dependency on gnome-keyring broke KDE-only and minimal installs
Preflight New --doctor flag on install.sh and claude-desktop Validates 15 checks: binaries, node version, CLI, /sessions, secret service, patches, stubs
Plugin listing Stub CustomPlugins_$_listAvailablePlugins IPC handler New asar calls this on session start; missing handler caused error spam
IPC discovery Dynamic eipc UUID extraction instead of hardcoded value UUID changes per asar build; hardcoding broke on every update
Linux UI Native window frames, icon extraction from .icns, titlebar patch Removes macOS titleBarOverlay/trafficLightPosition that rendered as blank space
Asar mounts Filter out /Applications and other macOS-only mounts Prevented EACCES errors from trying to symlink /Applications on Linux
AUR gnome-keyring moved from hard dep to optdep; launcher detects at runtime KDE/minimal users no longer forced to install gnome-keyring
--- ## Compatibility | Distro | Desktop | Status | |:-------|:--------|:-------| | **Arch Linux** | Hyprland / KDE / GNOME | Tested & Expected | | **Ubuntu 22.04+** | GNOME / X11 | Expected | | **Fedora 39+** | GNOME / KDE | Expected | | **Debian 12+** | Any | Expected | | **NixOS** | Any | Untested | | **openSUSE** | Any | Untested |
Known caveats - GNOME Wayland: no global shortcuts (upstream `xdg-desktop-portal-gnome` limitation) — set a custom shortcut in GNOME Settings instead. - Without a SecretService provider, credentials are stored on disk via `--password-store=basic`. - The `/sessions` root symlink requires `sudo` once during install.
--- ## Diagnostics Run preflight checks at any time: ```bash claude-desktop --doctor # from launcher ./install.sh --doctor # from repo ```
Example output ``` ========================================== Claude Desktop for Linux - Doctor Version: 3.0.2 ========================================== [OK] git: /usr/bin/git [OK] 7z: /usr/bin/7z [OK] node: ~/.local/share/mise/installs/node/24.13.0/bin/node [OK] npm: ~/.local/share/mise/installs/node/24.13.0/bin/npm [OK] electron: ~/.local/bin/electron [OK] asar: /usr/bin/asar [OK] bwrap: /usr/bin/bwrap [OK] Node.js version: v24 (>= 18) [OK] Claude binary: ~/.local/bin/claude [OK] /sessions symlink -> ~/.local/share/claude-cowork/sessions [OK] Secret service (org.freedesktop.secrets): available [OK] Extracted app: ~/.local/share/claude-desktop/linux-app-extracted [OK] Cowork patch: applied [OK] Swift stub: installed [OK] Python: 3.14.2 ========================================== 15 passed 0 warnings 0 failed ========================================== ```
--- ## Install / Upgrade
install.sh (recommended)
```bash # Fresh install git clone https://github.com/johnzfitch/claude-cowork-linux.git cd claude-cowork-linux && ./install.sh # Upgrade cd ~/.local/share/claude-desktop && git pull && ./install.sh ```
AUR (Arch Linux)
```bash yay -S claude-cowork-linux ```
curl pipe
```bash bash <(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/johnzfitch/claude-cowork-linux/master/install.sh) ```
--- ## Commits since v3.0.1 | Commit | Summary | |:-------|:--------| | `88d5607` | harden: distro compatibility and preflight diagnostics | | `a2bd3e5` | refactor: remove tracked ipc handler and align launcher runtime | | `bf59c0d` | refactor: track ipc-handler-setup.js at repo root | | `10710b6` | fix: implement CustomPlugins listAvailablePlugins via CLI | | `73c9d44` | fix: stub CustomPlugins to prevent spawn errors | | `da832b8` | fix: skip asar mounts in Cowork sessions | | `b1d9ae3` | fix: add Linux UI fixes to launcher | | `947e497` | fix: dynamic eipc UUID discovery and i18n path fix | | `83ea15c` | docs: fix outdated README information | | `d2925ec` | docs: update patch verification | | `2a25387` | chore: sync PKGBUILD with AUR | --- ## Contributors Thanks to the community members whose work landed in this release: - **[@Boermt-die-Buse](https://github.com/Boermt-die-Buse)** — Linux UI fixes: native window frames, titlebar patch, icon extraction ([PR #29](https://github.com/johnzfitch/claude-cowork-linux/pull/29)) - **[@JaPossert](https://github.com/JaPossert)** — Resources copy fix preventing startup crash ([PR #27](https://github.com/johnzfitch/claude-cowork-linux/pull/27)), Wayland global shortcuts report ([#28](https://github.com/johnzfitch/claude-cowork-linux/issues/28)) ---

releases.name: v3.0.1 — System Electron Fixes + Wayland Support
releases.body:
## System Electron Fixes + Wayland Support

This patch release fixes critical bugs affecting system Electron installations and adds Wayland global shortcut support.

### ![bug-exclamation-16x16](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/johnzfitch/claude-cowork-linux/master/.github/assets/icons/bug-exclamation-16x16.png) Bug Fixes

**System Electron i18n crash fixed** — Claude Desktop no longer crashes on startup with `ENOENT: resources/i18n/en-US.json`. The installer now copies all static assets from the DMG's `Contents/Resources/` directory. Tested on Fedora 43 with system Electron v22. (Incorporates #27)

**7z DMG extraction warnings tolerated** — Installation no longer fails on the `/Applications` symlink warning. Exit code 1 (warnings) is now tolerated; only exit codes 2+ cause failure. (Closes #25)

### ![lightbulb-16x16](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/johnzfitch/claude-cowork-linux/master/.github/assets/icons/lightbulb-16x16.png) Features

**Wayland global shortcuts** — Added `--enable-features=GlobalShortcutsPortal` for Wayland global shortcut support via `xdg-desktop-portal`. Works on KDE Plasma and Hyprland. **GNOME limitation:** `xdg-desktop-portal-gnome` has not implemented the GlobalShortcuts portal yet; GNOME users should set custom keyboard shortcuts in Settings. (Addresses #28)

### ![shield-security-protection-16x16](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/johnzfitch/claude-cowork-linux/master/.github/assets/icons/shield-security-protection-16x16.png) Security

**Cowork file preview security** — `FileSystem_$_readLocalFile` IPC handler now restricts reads to session directories only, enforces 10MB size cap, and validates paths to prevent traversal attacks. (#26)

---

### ![delivery-16x16](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/johnzfitch/claude-cowork-linux/master/.github/assets/icons/delivery-16x16.png) Installation

#### Via install.sh (recommended)

curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/johnzfitch/claude-cowork-linux/master/install.sh | bash
```

#### Via AUR (Arch Linux)

```

### ![sync-16x16](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/johnzfitch/claude-cowork-linux/master/.github/assets/icons/sync-16x16.png) Upgrade from v3.0.0

<details>

```bash
cd ~/.local/share/claude-desktop
git pull
./install.sh
```

**If installed via AUR:**
```bash

```

</details>

readme_html:
Claude Cowork on Linux

No macOS, no VM required.

Quick Start · How It Works · Manual Setup · Troubleshooting

Overview

Claude Cowork is a special Claude Desktop build that works inside a folder you point it at—it reads, writes, and organizes files there while it runs a plan. Cowork is currently a macOS-only preview backed by a sandboxed Linux VM; this repo reverse-engineers and stubs the macOS-native pieces so Cowork can run directly on Linux (x86_64)—no VM and no macOS required. The stub translates VM paths to host paths so Cowork points at the right files on Linux.

How it works:

Step Description
Stubbing Replace macOS-only native modules (@ant/claude-swift, @ant/claude-native) with JavaScript
Direct Execution Run the Claude Code binary directly (no VM needed—we're already on Linux!)
Path Translation Convert VM paths to host paths transparently
Platform Spoofing Send macOS headers so the server enables the feature

Status

  • Unofficial research preview: This is reverse-engineered and may break when Claude Desktop updates.
  • Linux support: Currently targets Linux x86_64. Wayland: auto-detected via $WAYLAND_DISPLAY / $XDG_SESSION_TYPE (Ozone backend).
  • Access: Requires a Claude account. The installer auto-downloads the Claude Desktop DMG; no macOS machine needed.

Compatibility

Distro Desktop Status Notes
Arch Linux Hyprland (Wayland) Tested Primary dev environment
Arch Linux KDE Plasma (Wayland) Expected KDE Wallet exposed via SecretService D-Bus
Arch Linux GNOME (Wayland) Expected Global shortcuts require manual DE config (GNOME lacks portal support)
Ubuntu 22.04+ GNOME / X11 Expected gnome-keyring provides SecretService
Fedora 39+ GNOME / KDE Expected May need p7zip-plugins for DMG extraction
Debian 12+ Any Expected p7zip-full in apt
NixOS Any Untested Electron + bwrap sandboxing may need extra config

Known caveats:

  • Wayland compositors that don't implement the GlobalShortcuts portal (GNOME) won't have global hotkey support -- set a custom shortcut in your DE settings instead.
  • If gnome-keyring or another SecretService provider isn't running, the launcher falls back to --password-store=basic (credentials stored on disk, not in a keyring).
  • The /sessions root symlink requires sudo once during install. If your distro restricts root symlinks differently, point it manually: sudo ln -s "$HOME/Library/Application Support/Claude/LocalAgentModeSessions/sessions" /sessions.

Run ./install.sh --doctor (or claude-desktop --doctor) after install to validate your environment.

Requirements

  • Linux x86_64 (tested on Arch Linux, kernel 6.18.7)
  • Node.js 18+ / npm
  • Electron (system package or npm global)
  • asar (npm install -g @electron/asar)
  • p7zip (to extract the macOS DMG; openSUSE uses 7zip instead)
  • bubblewrap (sandbox isolation)
  • Python 3.11+ (for auto-download and patches)
  • Claude Pro (or higher) subscription for Cowork access
  • Secret service provider (optional) -- gnome-keyring, KDE Wallet, or KeePassXC for secure credential storage. Without one, the launcher falls back to --password-store=basic.

Path Translation

The stub translates VM paths to host paths:

VM Path Host Path
/usr/local/bin/claude or claude Resolved via ~/.local/bin/claude, ~/.config/Claude/claude-code-vm/{version}/claude, or PATH
/sessions/... ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/LocalAgentModeSessions/sessions/...

Mount Symlinks

When you select a folder in Cowork, the stub creates symlinks to make it accessible at the expected VM path:

~/Library/Application Support/Claude/LocalAgentModeSessions/sessions/<session-name>/mnt/
├── <folder>  → /home/user/path/to/selected/folder (symlink)
├── .claude   → ~/.config/Claude/.../session/.claude (symlink)
├── .skills   → ~/.config/Claude/.../skills-plugin/... (symlink)
└── uploads/  (directory for file uploads)

The additionalMounts parameter from Claude Desktop provides the mapping between mount names and host paths.

[!NOTE] The Claude Code binary expects /sessions to exist. install.sh creates /sessions as a symlink into ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/LocalAgentModeSessions/sessions (requires sudo once) so you don't need a world-writable root directory.

How It Works

The app sends these headers to Anthropic's servers:

'Anthropic-Client-OS-Platform': 'darwin'
'Anthropic-Client-OS-Version': '14.0'

This makes the server think we're on macOS 14 (Sonoma), enabling Cowork features.

The platform-gate function (minified name changes per build — xPt() in v1.1.3963, wj() in older builds) checks if Cowork is supported. enable-cowork.py finds it automatically and replaces it to unconditionally return {status: "supported"}.

The original @ant/claude-swift uses Apple's Virtualization Framework. Our stub:

  • Implements the same API surface
  • Uses Node.js child_process to spawn real processes
  • Line-buffers JSON output for proper stream parsing
  • Translates VM paths to host paths

Key insight: The app calls Si() which returns module.default.vm, so methods must be on the vm object.

The app also expects @ant/claude-native (a macOS-specific native module). Our stub provides minimal compatibility so the app can start on Linux. For example, OAuth flows fall back to opening the system browser via xdg-open.

All EIPC handlers (session lifecycle, transcript management, feature flags) are registered by ipc-handler-setup.js, which is baked directly into app.asar. launch.sh repacks the asar automatically whenever stubs or frame-fix files change — no manual step required.

[!NOTE] Prior to v3.0.3, a separate linux-loader.js ran alongside the asar-baked handler via --require. It has been removed — ipc-handler-setup.js is now the sole IPC implementation.

On macOS, Cowork runs a Linux VM. On Linux, we skip the VM entirely and run the Claude Code binary directly on the host. This is actually simpler and faster!

The stub resolves the binary in priority order:

$CLAUDE_CODE_PATH                                    (explicit override)
~/.config/Claude/claude-code-vm/{version}/claude    (downloaded by Desktop)
~/.local/bin/claude                                  (npm/bun global)
~/.npm-global/bin/claude
/usr/local/bin/claude
/usr/bin/claude
/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/claude               (Linuxbrew system)
~/.linuxbrew/bin/claude                              (Linuxbrew user)
~/.local/share/mise/shims/claude                     (mise version manager)
~/.asdf/shims/claude                                 (asdf version manager)

Manual Setup

If the automated installer doesn't work, follow these steps:

The installer handles app.asar extraction automatically. For manual extraction or older unpacked versions:

# Extract DMG with 7z
7z x Claude-*.dmg -o/tmp/claude-extract

# Create app directory
mkdir -p linux-app-extracted

# For newer versions (app.asar):
if [ -f "/tmp/claude-extract/Claude/Claude.app/Contents/Resources/app.asar" ]; then
    npx --yes asar extract "/tmp/claude-extract/Claude/Claude.app/Contents/Resources/app.asar" linux-app-extracted
    # Copy unpacked files if they exist
    [ -d "/tmp/claude-extract/Claude/Claude.app/Contents/Resources/app.asar.unpacked" ] && \
        cp -r "/tmp/claude-extract/Claude/Claude.app/Contents/Resources/app.asar.unpacked/"* linux-app-extracted/
# For older versions (unpacked app/):
elif [ -d "/tmp/claude-extract/Claude/Claude.app/Contents/Resources/app" ]; then
    cp -r "/tmp/claude-extract/Claude/Claude.app/Contents/Resources/app/"* linux-app-extracted/
fi

# Cleanup
rm -rf /tmp/claude-extract

# Copy our stubs over the original modules
cp -r stubs/@ant/* linux-app-extracted/node_modules/@ant/

Run the cowork patch (auto-detects the minified function name):

python3 enable-cowork.py linux-app-extracted/.vite/build/index.js

# Create user session directory
mkdir -p "$HOME/Library/Application Support/Claude/LocalAgentModeSessions/sessions"
chmod 700 "$HOME/Library/Application Support/Claude/LocalAgentModeSessions/sessions"

# Create symlink (requires sudo once)
sudo ln -s "$HOME/Library/Application Support/Claude/LocalAgentModeSessions/sessions" /sessions

# System package (preferred)
# Arch: pacman -S electron
# Ubuntu/Debian: apt install electron
# Or via npm:
npm install -g electron @electron/asar

Troubleshooting

Check that the Cowork patch is present in linux-app-extracted/.vite/build/index.js:

grep -q 'cowork-patched' linux-app-extracted/.vite/build/index.js && echo "✓ Cowork patch applied" || echo "✗ Patch missing - run ./install.sh"

The patch replaces the platform-gate function to return {status:"supported"} unconditionally, enabling Cowork on Linux. The /*cowork-patched*/ marker indicates successful patching.

Create a symlink to user space instead of a world-writable directory:

mkdir -p "$HOME/Library/Application Support/Claude/LocalAgentModeSessions/sessions"
sudo ln -s "$HOME/Library/Application Support/Claude/LocalAgentModeSessions/sessions" /sessions

JSON parsing issue. The stub uses line buffering to send complete JSON objects. If this persists, check the trace log:

cat ~/.local/share/claude-cowork/logs/claude-swift-trace.log

Run claude-desktop --doctor first to check your environment. Then verify:

  1. The swift stub is properly loaded (check for [claude-swift-stub] LOADING MODULE in logs)
  2. The Claude binary exists at one of the resolved paths (~/.local/bin/claude, ~/.config/Claude/claude-code-vm/{version}/claude, etc.)
  3. You have a valid Claude account

Check stderr in the trace log for the actual error:

tail -50 ~/.local/share/claude-cowork/logs/claude-swift-trace.log

Common issues:

  • Missing /sessions symlink
  • Binary not found
  • Permission issues

This means the stub isn't exporting methods correctly. The app expects:

  • module.default.vm.setEventCallbacks() — NOT on the class directly

Ensure the stub has methods on the this.vm object, not just the class.

A previous instance may not have shut down cleanly, leaving a stale lock file. Clear it and relaunch:

rm -f ~/.config/Claude/SingletonLock ~/.config/Claude/SingletonSocket ~/.config/Claude/SingletonCookie
claude-desktop

This was fixed in v3.0.4 — the app now handles SIGTERM and window-close events gracefully. Update to the latest version to prevent it from recurring.

The app enables GlobalShortcutsPortal for Wayland global shortcut support via xdg-desktop-portal. This works on KDE Plasma and Hyprland but not on GNOME — xdg-desktop-portal-gnome has not implemented the GlobalShortcuts portal yet.

Workaround for GNOME Wayland users: Set a custom shortcut in GNOME Settings > Keyboard > Custom Shortcuts to launch claude-desktop.

Security

This project includes security hardening:

  • Command allowlist - Only vetted binary paths are accepted by vm.spawn(); unknown commands are rejected
  • Command injection prevention - Uses execFile() instead of exec()
  • Path traversal protection - Validates session paths with isPathSafe()
  • Environment filtering - Allowlist of safe environment variables
  • Secure permissions - Session directory uses 700, not 777
  • Symlink for /sessions - No world-writable directories
  • URL origin validation - Auth_$_doAuthInBrowser and AuthRequest.start() enforce Anthropic-only domains
  • OAuth compliance - BLOCKED_ENV_KEY_PATTERN + CREDENTIAL_EXEMPT_KEYS prevent token leakage to subprocesses
